title: Payena engleri
taxonomy: Payena engleri is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is a member of the genus Payena, which is native to tropical regions of Asia. The Sapotaceae family consists of trees and shrubs, commonly known as sapodilla or soapberry. This family is part of the larger order Ericales, which includes various plant species with similar characteristics. The classification of Payena engleri is based on its morphological features, such as its flowers, leaves, and fruit.

intro: Payena engleri, also known as Engler's payena, is a plant species in the family Sapotaceae. This plant is rich in various bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits. Payena engleri has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
